data.content: I'll start `dotnet watch test` for you — it will automatically re-run your tests whenever you save a file.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants hot reload set up with `dotnet watch test`. I can see this is an MSTest project targeting .NET 8. I'll start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so the user can iterate.

Let me first do a quick restore/build to make sure things are working, then start the watcher.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-5ce3b69b9bc143d4842c37393f0c1a5e && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test with hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content: The watcher is running but there's a build error — `ArgumentException` needs a `using System;` directive. Let me fix that so the watch loop works:
data.reasoningText:
There's a build error - `ArgumentException` needs `System` namespace. But the user said they have failing tests they want to iterate on, so let me just fix this build error quickly and let the watcher pick it up. Actually, the user asked me to set up hot reload, not fix their tests. Let me just point out the current state.

Wait, actually this is a compilation error, not a test failure. Let me fix this quickly so the watch loop works, then the user can iterate on their actual test failures.

Actually, looking more carefully: the test `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects 15.00m but the method returns 10.00m for "express". 

I need to add the `using System;` directive to clear the compilation error so the watch can run properly and surface the actual test failure.
